Alright, so here's what we're going to be applying this stuff to and that's my fancy way of saying here's what I'm going to be teaching you during the how to do ads part of this class. So we're going to be learning how to use your Facebooks and your Instagram which is essentially the same platform. Your YouTubes. We're going to use Google AdWords for PPC. We're not going to go super hardcore into PPC like you know with some types in a search term or whatever they'll see your ad. I'm also going to show you how to do omni channel retargeting so we can retarget people on stuff like LinkedIn and your snappy chats and your Twitter and any other platform that may pop up between the time I'm making this video and the time you actually get it because stuff changes all the time you know. But that's really what we're going to be doing here and it doesn't matter what the platform is or the channel or whatever you know. Essentially all an ad is and all we're ever doing is we're just showing a combination of the right words and the right images to the right people at the right time. And we could do that in a video like a YouTube ad for example or we could do it in you know typical Facebook ad that's got a picture and then some copy and stuff or we could go hog wild crazy and make a Facebook video ad that's got a video and it's got some copy. It's like double copy if you think about it you know or we could do it as a banner ad where you got a picture but then you got some copy on the picture as well. It doesn't matter it's ultimately images and words you know and so it's we're just going to show it to the right people at the right time and that's what makes ad works or makes ads work rather. So here's the main thing about ads. So this is really all that kind of matters and this is a deeper dive into what we talked about in the earlier video but in this one we're more specific here. So number one like the offer itself is really important you know nobody wants the thing you're selling it doesn't matter what combination of words and pictures we're using assuming we're not deceptive nobody's going to buy it right. So obviously we're going to take that into consideration. The after click experience is really really important as you're about to see as we go through all of this like it doesn't you know it's really not that hard to get someone to go to a website but the reason we spent so much time leading up to this working on your after click experience is because that matters that's what turns people into buyers you know and then of course another main thing is the creative is a fancy way of saying the ad itself but you know if you're in advertising and you have an ad agency like I do you have to use words like the creative to describe the ad because it makes you sound more professional but that's all that means is the ad itself and you know it's like obviously that's a pretty big deal there and then of course who you show it to and that is getting less important unless you deliberately target like the wrong people. So if we're selling steak and we deliberately target people who have shown interest in vegan related stuff we're probably going to do pretty badly but AI really takes care of a lot of the audience selection for us by AI I mean machine learning and artificial intelligence but it still matters and I'll show you how to do it and then of course the campaign objective and this is what a lot of you know ad people don't really teach you about for whatever reason and that's what you're telling the platform to do and we're about to take a deeper dive on that because there's like a ton of different things you can actually tell Facebook or YouTube or Google or whatever a lot of different stuff you can tell them to do which is kind of cool and then there's another thing like nobody ever talks about it that I've ever seen really and that's the bid strategy and that's how you're telling the platform to do it so there's different ways to tell Facebook or YouTube for example there's different ways to tell them how to go out and get you a customer and how you're willing to pay them for that and there's a time and place for all these different bid strategies and there's really really bad ass and it really helps you scale then for some reason not many people talk about it and it's great it's really awesome that they don't talk about it because it kind of gives us an unfair advantage and then there's another thing that people don't really talk about which is your media strategy and that's a fancy way of saying how everything fits together so let's take a deeper dive on those last three things there all right and we'll start with the campaign objective so what that is is that's what you're telling the platform to do right and so like you could say all right Facebook I got a video ad and I just want a bunch of people to see it and that would be a video views objective or you can say I got a website and I want a bunch of page views and that's that's really a Facebook thing Facebook will give you the option to choose between clicks to your website and page views and the difference is a page view is someone is when someone stays on your web page long enough for their pixel to load and tell Facebook that they actually viewed the page where a click is for someone to actually click the call to action button in your ad so a click in a website visitor or in a page view they're technically different things even though they're not I mean you're just sending someone to the website but like little subtle differences like that actually matter which is weird um engagement is another campaign objective you could tell Facebook like you know all right go out and get a bunch of people to engage with this ad and engage could just mean click on the ad read it comment it whatever um you could have a lead objective and I mean this in the context of Facebook lead formats Google also has like Google lead ads that are notoriously they've got a terrible reputation so we're not going to learn how to generate leads through that platform but Facebook lead ads where someone like enters their name and email address like directly on Facebook that's what I mean in this particular case and that's a perfectly valid way to get leads by the way it works um and I'll show you some campaigns where we do it and you'll see the results kind of crazy um and then there's a an objective called offers all right and so this is Facebook specific okay um so if you have like a candy store or something you could literally create a campaign for Facebook and you could run an offer that says come to the store by the 15th and use the use the offer code you know discount and we'll give you 25% off and like people will actually see that ad and they'll go to your store and they'll say all right you know I saw this ad on Facebook it said uses discount code and so like that that's a real thing now we're not going to go deep on that because number one I haven't really had a lot of success with that particular strategy number two most of us that do this are selling things online and then of course there's the one that most people are familiar with which is conversions and that's when you're saying all right Facebook or Google or whoever you know go out and get me someone who converts and a conversion could be an opt-in on your website or a purchase or or you know a registration or whatever all right but that's like if you choose the wrong campaign objective it doesn't matter if you do everything right the whole thing's shot to crap you know so it's really really important to know when to use each one of these campaign objectives um but oddly people don't really go into detail about it there's a lot of money and using things that aren't traditional campaign objectives like it's obviously we should set up campaigns for conversions but there's a strategy where it makes sense to set up campaigns for just clicks to a website believe it or not um that sometimes is better and so we'll go into that and it's it depends on where you're showing the ad and stuff right but that's what I mean by campaign objective the other thing that might have peter interests is this concept of bid strategy and this is really how you're telling the platform and you know I'm just gonna for now I'm gonna say Facebook but that's the same thing for all of them um this is how you're telling Facebook that you're willing to pay for whatever it is you want them to do so the most common one on Facebook and this is also very popular on Google and by Google I mean YouTube or you know PPC or display anything where you're advertising on any of Google's property it's something called max conversions right and so that's like the default which is hey just go out and get me as many conversions as possible and then you manually you know change your campaign based on how much stuff is costing and that's like the most popular and that's what everybody teaches but there's this whole other universe on YouTube ads and Google for example there's something called CPA where you can actually say okay I'm willing to pay you this much per sale or I'm willing to pay this much per opt-in and I'm willing to spend a gazillion dollars a day as long as you can get me sales or opt-ins for this much this is what I'm bidding right and so that that particular bid strategy CPA is for Google properties um but you know that's like that really eliminates a lot of risk especially if you know your numbers there's another one which is minimum row ads and this is really effective on Facebook it's not as effective on Google because their tracking is not as good um unfortunately not yet maybe that'll be fixed but on Facebook if like you've been running ads for a while and it kind of knows how much your return on ad spend is you can literally say okay run this ad and I will give you a million dollars a day as long as you're getting me this minimum return on ad spend and it won't actually spend your million dollars in a day it'll only spend as much as it can assuming it thinks it's going to get you that return on ad spend as a general rule um it freaking works you know it's crazy um there's another one called cost cap all right this is for Facebook specifically this is very similar to Google's CPA so cost cap means all right uh Facebook this is how much I'm willing to pay you for uh an opt-in or for a customer all right and so same thing is cost per action on Google this is called cost cap on Facebook and so it's freaking awesome and by the way if you say all right Facebook or Google you know I'm willing to spend a million dollars a day on as many customers you can get me for 20 per customer they will try their best to get you 20 per customer but you might end up paying like 23 or 24 per customer so understand that it's not guaranteed you know they're just doing the best they can but it's pretty dang close um I'll show you a screenshot in a minute there's bid cap which is on Facebook um and that's it's similar to cost cap but it just works a little bit differently and it's less effective in my opinion I usually run a campaign that'll test both of them and generally the bid cap one it just never works and often never spends any money but cost cap does all right um then you can actually bid and this is especially true for YouTube and Google display like banners you can say and you can actually do this on Facebook too especially if you do it like direct through Facebook and you're talking to the reps or something um I've had success with this on YouTube and Google I haven't on Facebook but you can do CPM bidding which is all right I'm just willing to pay you this much money per 1000 impressions that you give my ad now it's a risky proposition you know because a lot of times they're going to show your ad to junk traffic but this is where you'll start headed like if you know let's say you scale from you're spending 100 a day and now you're up to 50 000 a day and you're like how do I increase spend and still make a little bit of money you might test some CPM bidding uh as well and you might make a little bit because remember you know all advertising is all we're trying to do is multiply capital right so even if you increase your budget by a thousand dollars a day and it only brings you back an extra hundred dollars a day in net profit that's literally a hundred dollars a day you wouldn't have if you did that in the stock market if you made 10 per day on money invested in the stock market people would think you were the greatest financial genius in history right but CPM bidding that's that's a bid strategy that nobody really ever talks about C.P.V. this is popular on your YouTubes and this is we use this and we test this and this can be extremely effective especially in the right environments but this is where you're telling YouTube how much you're willing to pay to get someone to view specifically 30 seconds or more of one of your video ads right and so like these are just a couple these are just like the main different bid strategies and we're actually going to go deep on all of these and it's they're useful across every platform and let me give you an example by the way of that of that bid testing this is actually cost cap I just call it bid testing but it's a cost cap if you kind of see there in the title at the top there this is a recent test that I did I spent 971 dollars and I brought in 3700 bucks as you can see it made me a net profit of 2700 dollars and I tested several different amounts so I took the same audience and same ad I'll show you how to do all of this by the way so like we're going deep and really cool stuff you know we're not screwing around in this in this class but you can see I tested if you look at like these let's see if my cursor will show up oh it will awesome all right so this is one where I was like okay get me this is for my four dollar thing I was like get me buyers for seven dollars and then this one's give me buyers for ten dollars and this one's give me buyers for fourteen dollars and this one was get me buyers for twenty one dollars now what you don't see is the way I set this campaign up was I was like okay I'll bid 1400 dollars in total every day all right and I will bid a max amount that I'm willing to spend actually no I said a minimum amount that I'm willing to spend every day for whatever that was that was 1200 dollars now I've forgotten but essentially it spent less than I bid right so I was like hey you know spend a whole bunch of money if you're getting customers under 21 dollars or you know for 21 dollars it better and it didn't spend all the money because it couldn't get them for that much every day so this is a very risk a averse way to doing like getting customers it's really really cool and as you can see it it really works the disadvantages sometimes Facebook or if you're doing CPA on Google it doesn't think it could get your customers for that much that day so some days you won't spend anything you know so that's your only downside and so that's why we don't do like one-trick pony stuff here you know I'm going to show you how to do like all of these different things because if you really want your stuff to explode you're gonna there's gonna be a time and place to use everything now let's talk about your media strategy this is another thing that very few people ever actually talk about and that's how everything fits together in terms of like you know where do you run your ads when do you do retargeting where do you do retargeting what you know should you use Facebook or YouTube or AdWords or display and when and where do you do that so that combination is called your media strategy and generally speaking this is the way you do it we use Facebook and or YouTube as our primary top of funnel ad methodology right and so what that means is we're trying to get strangers to give us money or opt in depending on your sales process we're trying to get total strangers to do what we want them to do using Facebook and or YouTube right now some stuff might be YouTube specific you know and some folks some of y'all might be like I really just hate Facebook I want to focus on YouTube some folks might not want to mess with YouTube and some folks might be like me and be like dude I want to use both right but this is these are the primary platforms where you do your top of funnel and let me go get complete strangers to come into my little universe and that's almost always going to be a conversion objective where you're like telling them okay get me a customer get me an opt-in all right and then we do retargeting and man like wait till you see retargeting we can retarget on so many different levels it's crazy but our primary retargeting is going to be showing people who responded on Facebook and YouTube and by responding that could be clicking it could be engaging it could be watching something's like again we'll go deep dive in there there's like a zillion things and so we're going to show those people more ads on those platforms and those are also going to be conversion ads right and this is kind of where most people stop actually the first bullet is where most people stop which is like okay we're just going to run conversion ads and that's it and then some people go nuts and they're like okay I'm going to be advanced and I'm going to do a little bit of retargeting right and they're leaving a tremendous amount of money on the table because we can also retarget with display and that means banners and stuff and I call this secondary retargeting and the reason why is because all this stuff costs money right so the first thing we want to get profitable is we want to start making money on the top level right we want to show ads and strangers and we want to turn that into money with an acceptable amount of time and it's going to take a while especially if you've never done this before it's going to take a while for you to have enough people to start retargeting so then we start doing the retargeting and we want to make sure that's actually profitable and then once we kind of have that machine run in pretty good then we're like okay let's also retarget everybody with banners and stuff right and so that's like our secondary level of retargeting and notice that I've got we can run this as a conversion objective or we can run this as a traffic objective and this is stuff that people don't really talk about in some cases if your retargeting audience for banners is small but you're retargeting people who are obviously interested in your stuff it might make sense to say hey google or wherever we're going to show the banners let's just say it's google it might make sense to say hey google just send people to my damn website and you're going to bid on clicks instead of conversions that actually might be better might be more profitable to do that it might not right but like I'm going to show you how to figure it out and how to do both and really how to maximize every single thing and then our next level of retargeting is omni channel and that's icing on the cake and this is generally going to be conversion ads or click objectives depending again on which one makes sense you know so if we're showing like banners on linkedin or something it might make more sense to set that up as a retargeting thing if we're retargeting people who have like registered for a webinar and actually attended it doesn't matter if it's just a click objective you know like usually if we did that to strangers the platform would send us a bunch of junk clicks but if we're only doing that to people who have like already really gone deep into our funnel it makes sense and it's like a zillion times cheaper than doing it as a conversion ad you know and then our final layer on the cake here is branding and this is content videos and and like sending people to an article and this is typically like the icing on the cake as well so if you're if you're making everything profitable and you've got good profits coming in you might want to start reinvesting some of those profits into things like video views ads and these are ads where you're not actually you know trying to get a sale you're actually just getting content in front of people and indoctrinating them or you might be driving people to an article and then you can retarget the people that read that article or that article might have a pitch on there or whatever but it's really just your way of staying in front of people who are sort of on the fence right so most folks that teach this stuff most folks that do this stuff they they stay here in this little universe right here but generally speaking like the you know this a fortune can be made and these three things right here if we do these two properly and you know and again all this stuff is it's just us multiplying capital you know so even if you're like let's say you you're and you know this might seem crazy at the moment but let's say you're you get up to where you're spending $30,000 a day um and you're like I want to multiply more capital you might find that spending like a thousand dollars a day on branding videos causes more people to go to google and type in your domain name and you might start getting more sales and uh that's still multiplying capital and you know you might only get 100 extra dollars in profit every day but it's still profit you know so I always tell clients I'm like well what would you rather have you know a 10 return on 10 dollars or a 10 return on 10 million dollars is like if we're multiplying capital is multiplies a freaking capital right so that's what media strategy means so on a 30,000 foot level this is the stuff we're going to be working on and doing throughout the duration of this course and we're going to be doing all this stuff on all these different channels all right so the good news is at the end of the day if it's starting to sound complicated it's really not because all we're talking about is showing a combination of the right words and the right images to the right people at the right time that's it that's all we're doing and if we think about it that way if you adhere to the principles I taught in the last video then you're going to be good all right so without further ado why don't we start here which is because this is everybody's favorite why don't we start with facebook and let's start building a top of funnel conversion campaign on facebook you're ready let's do it i'll see you in the next video
